zh.lifeSummary: Emma Tew是Hugh Tew的女兒，於大約1244年出生在英國奧可福爾鄧斯 圖。她於大約1274年與Richard de Lyons在英國奧可福爾鄧斯 圖結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1312年在英國奧可福爾鄧斯 圖享年69歲，埋葬在英國奧可福爾阿德波利聖 馬也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Tew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: nickname for a fat man, from tew ‘plump’.

English: habitational name from a place in Oxfordshire (Great, Little and Duns Tew), named with an Old English tīewe ‘row, lengthy object’, referring to the ridge on which these places are situated, or from tīwe ‘meeting place, court’. The surname has been established in Ireland since the 16th century.
Chinese: Teochew, Hokkien, and Taiwanese form of the surnames 張 (see   Zhang   1) and 趙 (see   Zhao   1).

variants.name: Zhang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Wei, Hong, Li, Jian, Ming, Yi, Jing, Hui, Yan, Fan, Xin, Ping, Min, Hao, Quan, Lan, Tian, Chong, Da, Huan, Xuan, You, Chang, Shen, Yiming.

Mandarin form of the surname 張, meaning ‘to draw a bow’ in ancient Chinese: (i) for some families, it is traced back to Xie Zhang (解張), whose style name was Zhang Hou (張侯) (‘Marquis Zhang’), a noble in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). (ii) this surname is also borne by descendants of Hui, a grandson of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). Hui was given the title Gong Zheng, denoting an official in charge of making bows. (張 Zhang means ‘to draw a bow’ in ancient Chinese). (iii) from the name of Zhang Liao (張遼), an official in the state of Wei in northern China during the Three Kingdoms period (220–280 AD ). (iv) the surname is also traced back to Long Youna, chief of one of the minority ethnic groups (located mainly in present-day Sichuan, Guizhou, and Yunnan provinces) during the Three Kingdoms period (220–280 AD ), who was given the Chinese surname Zhang (張) by Zhuge Liang, the prime minister of the state of Shu.

nameMeanings.name: Emma
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of compound names such as Ermintrude containing the word erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, but its popularity in medieval England was greatly enhanced by the fact that it had been borne by the mother of Edward the Confessor, herself a Norman. In modern times, it was only in moderate use early in the 20th century but rose sharply in favour in the 1970s and has since remained perennially popular.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: Emma Tew was born about 1244, in Duns Tew, Oxfordshire, England as the daughter of Hugh Tew. She married Richard de Lyons about 1274, in Duns Tew, Oxfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1312, in Duns Tew, Oxfordshire, England, at the age of 69, and was buried in St Mary's Church, Adderbury, Oxfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Emma Tew est née environ 1244, à Duns Tew, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, fille de Hugh Tew. Elle a épousé Richard de Lyons environ 1274, à Duns Tew, Oxfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée en 1312, à Duns Tew, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à St Mary's Church, Adderbury, Oxfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Emma Tew wurde um 1244 in Duns Tew, Oxfordshire, England, als Tochter von Hugh Tew geboren. Sie hat um 1274 in Duns Tew, Oxfordshire, England Richard de Lyons geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb 1312 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Duns Tew, Oxfordshire, England und wurde in St Mary's Church, Adderbury, Oxfordshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Emma Tewは約1244年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアダンス テウでHugh Tewの娘として生まれた。約1274年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアダンス テウでRichard de Lyonsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1312年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアダンス テウで69歳で死去し，イギリスオクスフォードシュアイアアッドエルブリースト マリ'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St Mary's Church, Adderbury, Oxfordshire: 영국 스퍼드실르 애더버이 스트 마이'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Emma Tew(은)는 대략 1244년 영국 스퍼드실르 던스 튜에서 Hugh Tew의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1274년 영국 스퍼드실르 던스 튜에서 Richard de Lyons(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1312년 영국 스퍼드실르 던스 튜에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스퍼드실르 애더버이 스트 마이'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Richard de Lyons aproximadamente 1274, em Duns Tew, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. ru.St Mary's Church, Adderbury, Oxfordshire: ст мэри'с чурч, аддирбури, оксфордшир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Emma Tew родилась около 1244 г. в местности дунс тю, оксфордшир, Англия. Её отца звали Hugh Tew. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard de Lyons около 1274 г. в местности дунс тю, оксфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в 1312 г. в местности дунс тю, оксфордшир, Англия в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности ст мэри'с чурч, аддирбури, оксфордшир, Англия.
